message:
Enhance TUI with multi-mode filtering, pagination, and rich theming

This commit substantially upgrades the terminal user interface with a
full-featured interactive experience including multiple view modes,
sophisticated filtering, smooth pagination, and a polished theme system.

Theme system enhancements (components/theme.rs):
- Expanded color palette with semantic naming (primary, secondary, accent,
  muted, error, warning, success)
- Role-specific message styling (user vs agent differentiation)
- Highlight states for selected items, active filters, search matches
- Border styles for panels, popups, and focus indicators
- Status bar gradients for visual hierarchy
- Consistent spacing and padding constants for layout uniformity
- Dark mode optimized with ANSI 256-color support

TUI functionality upgrades (tui.rs):
- Multi-mode operation: List, Detail, Search, Filter, Help
- List mode:
  - Scrollable conversation list with vim-like navigation (j/k, PageUp/Down)
  - Visual selection indicator with smooth cursor movement
  - Real-time metadata display (agent, workspace, timestamp, message count)
  - Sort options (date, agent, workspace) with toggle hotkeys
  - Lazy loading with configurable page size (default 50 items)
- Detail mode:
  - Full conversation view with message-by-message rendering
  - Syntax-aware content display with role-based coloring
  - Message metadata (author, timestamp, token estimates)
  - Scrollable viewport for long conversations
  - Breadcrumb navigation (back to list with ESC or 'q')
- Search mode:
  - Real-time query input with visual feedback
  - Debounced search execution (300ms delay)
  - Result highlighting with match context
  - Search history with up/down arrow recall
  - Query syntax hints (FTS5 operators, boolean logic)
- Filter mode:
  - Multi-dimensional filtering: agent, workspace, date range
  - Checkbox-style filter selection with visual indicators
  - Cumulative filter composition (AND logic across dimensions)
  - Active filter badge display in status bar
  - Clear all filters hotkey
- Help mode:
  - Comprehensive keybinding reference
  - Context-sensitive help (varies by active mode)
  - Command palette with search
  - Exit with '?' or ESC

Pagination and performance:
- Virtualized rendering (only visible items materialized)
- Offset-based SQL queries for efficient large dataset handling
- Configurable page size via environment variable
- Visual page indicators (1/10, etc.) in status bar
- Smooth scrolling with momentum (hold key for rapid movement)

Footer and status bar (mod.rs updates):
- Dynamic hotkey legend based on current mode
- Breadcrumb trail for navigation context
- Active filter/search summary
- Database stats (total conversations, indexed messages)
- Real-time mode indicator with visual distinction

Keyboard shortcuts:
- Navigation: j/k, arrows, PageUp/Down, Home/End
- Modes: '/', 'f', '?', ESC (return to list)
- Actions: Enter (select), 'd' (detail), 's' (search), 'q' (quit)
- Filters: 'c' (clear), 'a' (agent), 'w' (workspace), 't' (time)
- Sort: '1' (date), '2' (agent), '3' (workspace)

Integration with data layer:
- Uses ConversationView from data.rs for detail mode
- Efficient SQL queries via storage abstraction
- Lazy message loading (detail view only fetches when needed)

Accessibility and UX polish:
- High-contrast mode support
- Terminal size adaptation (graceful degradation on small terminals)
- Loading spinners for async operations
- Error message display with actionable guidance
- Consistent visual language across all modes

This represents a major milestone in usability, transforming the TUI from
a basic conversation list into a fully interactive search and browse
interface comparable to dedicated log viewers.

Complements the dual-backend search (Tantivy + SQLite FTS5) introduced in
prior commits by providing a rich UI for exploring results.
